intro: Carthamodes pectinata, also known as Desert Safflower or False Safflower, is a thistle-like perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the deserts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve heart health, and reduce oxidative stress.
